Class EfsDispatcher

java.lang.Object
org.efs.dispatcher.EfsDispatcher
All Implemented Interfaces:
IEfsDispatcher
public final class EfsDispatcher extends Object implements IEfsDispatcher
This class forwards events to agents and has each agent process those events in a way that is effectively single-threaded. See the package info for detailed explanation on how dispatchers, agents, and events work together to accomplish this.

Creating Dispatchers

There are three ways to create dispatchers in an application:
  1. loading that typesafe configuration file manually via loadDispatchersConfigFile(File),
  2. manually creating an EfsDispatchersConfig and loading it via loadDispatchers(EfsDispatchersConfig) (not recommend - next option preferred), and
  3. use EfsDispatcher.Builder to programmatically create dispatchers. See Builder class javadoc for detailed explanation on building a dispatcher.

Note: dispatchers must be created and started when the application starts and are run for application's duration. Dispatchers must be up and running prior to create any IEfsAgent instances which are dependent on specific dispatchers. If an agent is registered to an undefined dispatcher, this results in a thrown IllegalArgumentException due to an unknown dispatcher.

Also note that there is no way is provided to stop a dispatcher once started.

Author:
Charles W. Rapp
See Also:
  • EfsDispatcherConfig
  • EfsDispatcher.Builder

  • Nested Class Summary

    Nested Classes
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    final class 
    EfsDispatcher.AgentStats
    Immutable class containing aggregated EfsDispatcherThread.AgentStats providing a snapshot of efs dispatcher's performance.
    static final class 
    EfsDispatcher.Builder
    Builder for constructing EfsDispatcher instance.
    static enum 
    EfsDispatcher.DispatcherState
    A dispatcher is either stopped, started, or failed to start.
    final class 
    EfsDispatcher.DispatcherStats
    Contains EfsDispatcherThread.DispatcherThreadStats for each dispatcher thread.
    static enum 
    EfsDispatcher.DispatcherType
    Enumerates the supported efs dispatcher thread types.

    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    static final int
    DEFAULT_EVENT_QUEUE_CAPACITY
    Default dispatcher event queue capacity is 1024.
    static final int
    DEFAULT_MAX_EVENTS
    The default maximum events per call out is 4.
    static final Duration
    DEFAULT_PARK_TIME
    The default park time after spinning is 1 microsecond.
    static final int
    DEFAULT_PRIORITY
    Default dispatcher has Thread.NORM_PRIORITY priority.
    static final int
    DEFAULT_RUN_QUEUE_CAPACITY
    Default dispatcher run queue capacity is 64.
    static final int
    DEFAULT_SPIN_LIMIT
    The default spin limit is 2500000 calls to Queue.poll() before parking or yielding.
    static final ThreadType
    DEFAULT_THREAD_TYPE
    Default dispatcher thread type is ThreadType.BLOCKING.
    static final String
    DISPATCHER_KEY
    Key "dispatcher" sets dispatcher method.
    static final String
    DISPATCHER_NAME_KEY
    Key "dispatcherName" sets dispatcher thread name prefix.

    DISPATCHER_TYPE_KEY
    Key "dispatcherType" sets dispatcher thread type.
    static final String
    EVENT_QUEUE_CAPACITY_KEY
    Key "eventQueueCapacity" sets efs agent maximum event queue capacity.
    static final String
    INVALID_NAME

    Key "maxEvents" set agent maximum events per call out.

    Event queue minimum allowed size is 2.

    THREAD_TYPE_KEY
    Key "threadType" sets dispatcher thread type.

    • DEFAULT_THREAD_TYPE

      public static final ThreadType DEFAULT_THREAD_TYPE
      Default dispatcher thread type is ThreadType.BLOCKING.

      public static final int DEFAULT_PRIORITY
      Default dispatcher has Thread.NORM_PRIORITY priority.

      Default dispatcher event queue capacity is 1024.

      Event queue minimum allowed size is 2.

      Default dispatcher run queue capacity is 64.

      public static final int DEFAULT_SPIN_LIMIT
      The default spin limit is 2500000 calls to Queue.poll() before parking or yielding.
      See Also:

      public static final Duration DEFAULT_PARK_TIME
      The default park time after spinning is 1 microsecond.

      public static final int DEFAULT_MAX_EVENTS
      The default maximum events per call out is 4.

      • Constant Field Values

      public static final String DISPATCHER_NAME_KEY
      Key "dispatcherName" sets dispatcher thread name prefix.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • NUM_THREADS_KEY

      public static final String NUM_THREADS_KEY
      Key "numThreads" sets dispatcher thread count;
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • THREAD_TYPE_KEY

      public static final String THREAD_TYPE_KEY
      Key "threadType" sets dispatcher thread type.

      The default value is DEFAULT_THREAD_TYPE.

      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • SPIN_LIMIT_KEY

      public static final String SPIN_LIMIT_KEY
      Key "spinLimit" sets thread spin limit for spin+park or ThreadType.SPINYIELD thread types. This setting defines the number of times the dispatcher thread may call Queue.poll() before parking or yielding.

      This value must be > zero.

      Default values is DEFAULT_SPIN_LIMIT.

      This property is ignored is the thread type is not spin+park or spin+yield.

      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • PARK_TIME_KEY

      public static final String PARK_TIME_KEY
      Key "parkTime" sets park time for spin+park dispatcher thread. This setting specifies nanosecond park time taken between poll spin cycles.

      This value must be > zero.

      Default values is DEFAULT_PARK_TIME.

      This property is ignored is the thread type is not spin+park.

      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • PRIORITY_KEY

      public static final String PRIORITY_KEY
      Key "priority" sets dispatcher thread priority.

      The default value is Thread.NORM_PRIORITY.

      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • MAX_EVENTS_KEY

      public static final String MAX_EVENTS_KEY
      Key "maxEvents" set agent maximum events per call out. When a agent hits this limit, that agent is placed back on the run queue.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • DISPATCHER_TYPE_KEY

      public static final String DISPATCHER_TYPE_KEY
      Key "dispatcherType" sets dispatcher thread type. This property allows efs events to be executed on supported third-party threads.

      The default value is EfsDispatcher.DispatcherType.EFS.

      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • DISPATCHER_KEY

      public static final String DISPATCHER_KEY
      Key "dispatcher" sets dispatcher method.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • EVENT_QUEUE_CAPACITY_KEY

      public static final String EVENT_QUEUE_CAPACITY_KEY
      Key "eventQueueCapacity" sets efs agent maximum event queue capacity. This value is used only for non-blocking dispatcher thread types.

      Default value is DEFAULT_EVENT_QUEUE_CAPACITY.

      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • RUN_QUEUE_CAPACITY_KEY

      public static final String RUN_QUEUE_CAPACITY_KEY
      Key "runQueueCapacity" sets run queue maximum size. Only used for non-blocking thread types.

      Default value is DEFAULT_RUN_QUEUE_CAPACITY.

      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • INVALID_NAME

      public static final String INVALID_NAME
      Invalid name IllegalArgumentException message is "name is either null, empty, or blank string".
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • MISSING_AGENT_NAME

      public static final String MISSING_AGENT_NAME
      Missing agent name message is "agent name is either null, empty, or blank string".
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

      Invalid dispatcher type NullPointerException message is "type is null".

    • dispatch

      public void dispatch(org.efs.dispatcher.EfsAgent agent)
      Enqueues given agent to this dispatcher's run queue.
      Specified by:
      dispatch in interface IEfsDispatcher
      Parameters:
      agent - enqueue this efs agent.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if dispatcher run queue is full and agent was not enqueued.

      public String toString()
      Returns text containing efs dispatcher settings.

      efs dispatcher settings in text.

      underlying thread type.

      public int priority()
      Returns priority of dispatcher threads.
      Returns:
      dispatcher thread priorty.

    • isRegistered

      public static boolean isRegistered(IEfsAgent agent)
      Returns true if given agent is currently registered with an efs dispatcher and false if not registered.
      Parameters:
      agent - check if this agent is registered with an efs dispatcher.
      Returns:
      true if agent is currently registered.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if agent is null.
      See Also:
      • register(IEfsAgent, String)
      • deregister(IEfsAgent)

    • agent

      @Nullable public static IEfsAgent agent(String agentName)
      Returns efs agent instance with the given name. If there is no such named agent, then returns null.
      Parameters:
      agentName - find agent with this name.
      Returns:
      efs agent named agentName.

    • dispatcher

      @Nullable public static String dispatcher(IEfsAgent agent)
      Returns agent's assigned dispatcher's name. If agent is not currently registered, then returns null.
      Parameters:
      agent - registered agent.
      Returns:
      agent's assigned dispatcher's name.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if agent is null.

    • isDispatcher

      public static boolean isDispatcher(String name)
      Returns true if given name is a known dispatcher and false otherwise.
      Parameters:
      name - unique dispatcher name.
      Returns:
      true if name is a known dispatcher.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if name is either null or an empty string.

    • dispatcherNames

      public static List<String> dispatcherNames()
      Returns an immutable copy of dispatcher names.
      Returns:
      dispatcher names list.

    • getDispatcher

      @Nullable public static IEfsDispatcher getDispatcher(String name)
      Returns dispatcher associated with the given name. Returns null if no dispatcher is associated with the name.
      Parameters:
      name - dispatcher name.
      Returns:
      dispatcher named name.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if name is null or an empty string.

    • performanceStats

      @Nullable public static EfsDispatcher.DispatcherStats performanceStats(String name)
      Returns named dispatcher's performance statistics snapshot. This snapshot is not dynamically updated. This method must be called to receive changes in dispatcher's performance.
      Parameters:
      name - dispatcher name.
      Returns:
      named dispatcher's performance.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if name is either null or an empty string.

    • register

      public static void register(IEfsAgent agent, String dispatcherName)
      Registers agent with named dispatcher. Allows programmatic matching of agents with dispatcher.
      Parameters:
      agent - assign this agent with named dispatcher.
      dispatcherName - unique dispatcher name.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if agent is null.
      IllegalArgumentException - if agent.name() returns a null, empty, or blank string or dispatcher name is null, empty, or blank string. or an unknown dispatcher.
      IllegalStateException - if agent is already registered.
      See Also:
      • dispatch(Runnable, IEfsAgent)
      • dispatch(Consumer, IEfsEvent, IEfsAgent)
      • deregister(IEfsAgent)

    • dispatch

      public static <E extends IEfsEvent> void dispatch(Consumer<E> callback, E event, IEfsAgent agent)
      Forwards event to agent using given callback.

      callback should reference a method in agent but this is not enforced.

      Note: an IllegalStateException if agent is not currently registered with a dispatcher but if agent is de-registered simultaneously with this call, it is possible for the event to be either posted to agent event queue or quietly dropped. This behavior is not a problem since events are not normally expected to be delivered after de-registration (but may still happen).

      Type Parameters:
      E - event class.
      Parameters:
      callback - Consumer instance used to forward event to agent.
      event - send this event to agent.
      agent - efs agent to receive given event.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if either callback, event, or agent is null.
      IllegalArgumentException - if agent name is either null, empty, or blank string.
      IllegalStateException - if agent is not registered or if agent's event queue is full preventing event from being enqueued.
      See Also:
      • register(IEfsAgent, String)
      • dispatch(Runnable, IEfsAgent)
      • deregister(IEfsAgent)

    • dispatch

      public static <E extends IEfsEvent> void dispatch(EfsDispatchTarget<E> target, E event)
      Forwards event to agent and callback contained in dispatch target.

      Note: an IllegalStateException if agent is not currently registered with a dispatcher but if agent is de-registered simultaneously with this call, it is possible for the event to be either posted to agent event queue or quietly dropped. This behavior is not a problem since events are not normally expected to be delivered after de-registration (but may still happen).

      Type Parameters:
      E - event class.
      Parameters:
      target - contains non-null callback and agent to which event is dispatched.
      event - send this event to targeted agent.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if either target or event is null.
      IllegalStateException - if agent is not registered or if agent's event queue is full preventing event from being enqueued.

    • dispatch

      public static void dispatch(Runnable task, IEfsAgent agent)
      Executes given task in agent dispatcher inline with dispatched tasks. This means task is performed in agent's virtually single threaded manner.
      Parameters:
      task - perform this task inline with events.
      agent - run task inline with this agent's events.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if either task or agent is null.
      IllegalArgumentException - if agent name is either null, empty, or blank string.
      IllegalStateException - if agent's event queue is full preventing task from being enqueued.
      See Also:
      • register(IEfsAgent, String)
      • dispatch(Consumer, IEfsEvent, IEfsAgent)
      • deregister(IEfsAgent)

    • deregister

      public static void deregister(IEfsAgent agent)
      De-registers agent from its dispatcher.

      Note: it is still possible that an event will still be delivered to agent after this call due to this de-registration and event dispatch occurring on different, concurrent threads. Therefore care must be taken to handle this possibility.

      Parameters:
      agent - de-register this agent from its dispatcher.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if agent is null.
      IllegalArgumentException - if agent name is either null, empty, or blank string.
      See Also:
      • register(IEfsAgent, String)
      • dispatch(Runnable, IEfsAgent)
      • dispatch(Consumer, IEfsEvent, IEfsAgent)

    • builder

      public static EfsDispatcher.Builder builder(String name)
      Returns a new dispatcher configuration builder. It is strongly that a new builder be used for each new dispatcher configuration.
      Parameters:
      name - unique dispatcher name.
      Returns:
      a new dispatcher configuration builder.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if dispatcherName is either null, empty, or a blank string.
      IllegalStateException - if there already is a dispatcher named dispatcherName.

    • loadDispatchersConfigFile

      public static void loadDispatchersConfigFile(File configFile)
      Loads dispatcher definitions from named file and then creates and starts dispatchers based on those definitions.

      Note: if this method is called from application code, then it must be called prior to creating any agent which use the dispatchers defined in fileName. If not, agent registration will fail with an IllegalArgumentException due to unknown dispatcher.

      See EfsDispatchersConfig for an example dispatchers configuration file in typesafe HOCON format.

      Parameters:
      configFile - file containing efs dispatcher definitions.
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if configFile is null.
      IllegalArgumentException - if:
      • configFile is a non-existent file,
      • configFile is not a regular file,
      • configFile cannot be read.
      com.typesafe.config.ConfigException - if fileName contains an invalid dispatcher configuration.
      ThreadStartException - if an efs dispatcher thread fails to start.
      See Also:
      • EfsDispatchersConfig

    • loadDispatchers

      public static void loadDispatchers(EfsDispatchersConfig dispatchersConfig)
      Loads dispatcher definitions from named file and then creates and starts dispatchers based on those definitions.

      Note: if this method is called from application code, then it must be called prior to creating any agent which use the dispatchers defined in dispatchersConfig. If not, agent registration will fail with an IllegalArgumentException due to unknown dispatcher.

      Parameters:
      dispatchersConfig - dispatcher configurations.
      Throws:
      ThreadStartException - if an efs dispatcher thread fails to start.

    • addDispatcher

      public static void addDispatcher(String dispatcherName, IEfsDispatcher dispatcher)
      Adds a mocked dispatcher with the given name to dispatchers map.
      Parameters:
      dispatcherName - dispatcher name.
      dispatcher - dispatcher instance.
